title: $:/plugins/rimir/doc-template
name: doc template
description: Branded documentation system for rimir plugins
author: rimir
list: readme documentation settings hub changelog license
text:

","type":"text/x-markdown"},"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/doc-template/README.md":{"title":"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/doc-template/README.md","text":"# Doc Template

> Branded documentation system for rimir plugins

Renders plugin documentation with a consistent layout featuring a dark header, tab navigation, breadcrumbs, and an auto-discovery hub that lists all documented plugins.

## Key features

* **Auto-discovery hub** -- card grid grouped by category, finds all documented plugins automatically
* **Branded page layout** -- header, breadcrumb, tabs, and footer for consistent presentation
* **Field-based discovery** -- no tags needed, uses `rdt.*` fields for metadata
* **Callout boxes** -- four types: info, warning, tip, danger
* **In-place navigation** -- seamless browsing when accessed from the hub
* **Related plugins footer** -- links to related plugins at the bottom of each page

## Prerequisites

* theme plugin (for CSS classes)

## Quick start

Create `doc-overview.tid` in your plugin with `rdt.plugin-key`, `rdt.section`, `rdt.plugin-name`, `rdt.plugin-subtitle`, `rdt.hub-category` fields. The version is read automatically from `plugin.info`. Transclude `$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page` and fill the `body` slot. The hub auto-discovers it.

## Plugin Library

Install from the [rimir plugin library](https://rimir-cc.github.io/tw-plugin-library/) via *Control Panel → Plugins → Get more plugins*.

## Demo

Try this plugin in the [live demo wiki](https://rimir-cc.github.io/tw-demo/).

## License

MIT -- see [LICENSE.md](LICENSE.md)

"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/architecture":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/architecture","created":"20260305110542029","modified":"20260305110542029","rdt.order":"2","rdt.plugin-key":"doc-template","rdt.plugin-name":"doc template","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Branded documentation system for rimir plugins","rdt.section":"Architecture","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Discovery mechanism

Doc tiddlers are discovered entirely via fields — no tags required:

* `rdt.section` — identifies a tiddler as a doc section (e.g., `Overview`, `Architecture`)
* `rdt.plugin-key` — groups sections by plugin (e.g., `runner`, `realms`)
* `rdt.order` — controls tab ordering within a plugin

The hub filter: `[all[shadows+tiddlers]rdt.section[Overview]has[rdt.hub-category]]` finds all Overview tiddlers that declare a hub category.

!! Hub state machine

The hub (`hub.tid`) has two modes controlled by the state tiddler `$:/state/rimir/doc-template/hub/selected`:

* ''Card grid'' (default) — when state is empty, renders the category-grouped card grid
* ''Plugin detail'' — when state holds a doc tiddler title, transcludes that tiddler's page

This allows in-place navigation without opening new tiddlers.

!! rdt-hub-context variable

When a doc page is rendered from the hub, `rdt-hub-context` is set to `"yes"`. This changes navigation behaviour:

* ''Hub context'' (`rdt-hub-context = "yes"`) — breadcrumb and footer use `<$button>` to update hub state (in-place navigation)
* ''Direct context'' (no hub context) — breadcrumb and footer use `<$link>` to open tiddlers normally

!! Page template

`templates/page.tid` renders the full doc page layout:

# Checks `rdt-rendering` variable — if `"yes"`, outputs raw `<$slot $name="body">` to prevent infinite recursion when tab content is transcluded
# Sets variables from `currentTiddler` fields: `currentPluginKey`, `currentPluginName`, `currentPluginVersion`, `currentPluginSubtitle`
# Builds `tabState` per plugin: `$:/state/rimir/doc-template/tab/<plugin-key>`
# Resolves `selectedTab` — uses stored state if valid, falls back to `currentTiddler`
# Renders header, breadcrumb, tab bar, content (via `<$transclude $tiddler=<<selectedTab>>>`), and footer

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="warning">
<$fill $name="body">
The `rdt-rendering` guard is critical. Without it, transcluding a tab's content would re-render the full page template, creating infinite recursion.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

!! Category system

Categories are fully data-driven via three fields on Overview tiddlers:

* `rdt.hub-category` — machine key (e.g., `infrastructure`)
* `rdt.hub-category-name` — display name (e.g., `Infrastructure`)
* `rdt.hub-order` — sort order within the hub

The hub uses `each[rdt.hub-category]` to deduplicate categories, then lists plugins within each category sorted by `rdt.plugin-name`.

!! Macro

`rdt.plugin-card(title)` — a single-parameter procedure that reads `rdt.plugin-name`, `rdt.plugin-version`, and `rdt.plugin-subtitle` from the given tiddler and renders a clickable card button.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/dev-workflow":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/dev-workflow","created":"20260305110542029","modified":"20260305110542029","rdt.order":"3","rdt.plugin-key":"doc-template","rdt.plugin-name":"doc template","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Branded documentation system for rimir plugins","rdt.section":"Dev Workflow","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Adding docs to a new plugin

# Create doc tiddler files in the plugin directory (e.g., `mywiki/plugins/my-plugin/`)
# Each file needs the standard `rdt.*` fields (see field reference below)
# The Overview tiddler needs the additional hub fields (`rdt.hub-category`, `rdt.hub-order`, `rdt.hub-category-name`)
# Create a `readme.tid` with a `$tiddler` wrapper to set `currentTiddler` before transcluding the Overview

!! File naming convention

Files on disk follow the pattern `doc-<section>.tid`. TiddlyWiki resolves the title from the `title` field inside the file:

|!Disk file |!Tiddler title |
|`doc-overview.tid` |`$:/plugins/rimir/<slug>/doc/overview` |
|`doc-architecture.tid` |`$:/plugins/rimir/<slug>/doc/architecture` |
|`doc-faq.tid` |`$:/plugins/rimir/<slug>/doc/faq` |

!! Field reference

|!Field |!Required on |!Description |
|`rdt.plugin-key` |All doc tiddlers |Plugin identifier (e.g., `runner`, `realms`) |
|`rdt.section` |All doc tiddlers |Section name — used as tab label |
|`rdt.order` |All doc tiddlers |Tab sort order (numeric, 1 = first) |
|`rdt.plugin-name` |All doc tiddlers |Display name for header and cards |
|`rdt.plugin-subtitle` |All doc tiddlers |Subtitle for header and card description |
|`rdt.hub-category` |Overview only |Category key for hub grouping |
|`rdt.hub-order` |Overview only |Sort order within hub |
|`rdt.hub-category-name` |Overview only |Display name for category heading |

!! Adding a new category

No code changes needed. Just set the three hub fields on any Overview tiddler:

* `rdt.hub-category: my-category`
* `rdt.hub-category-name: My Category`
* `rdt.hub-order: 5`

The hub will automatically create a new category section.

!! Restart rules

|!Change |!Restart needed? |
|Edit any `.tid` file in a plugin |Yes — plugin shadow tiddlers |
|Edit `hub.tid`, `macros.tid` |Yes — plugin shadow tiddlers |
|Edit `styles.tid` |Yes — plugin shadow tiddlers |
|Edit `templates/page.tid` or `templates/callout.tid` |Yes — plugin shadow tiddlers |

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="tip">
<$fill $name="body">
All doc-template files are plugin shadow tiddlers. Any change requires a server restart and browser hard-refresh.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

!! Version history

|!Version |!Changes |
|0.1.0 |Initial release — hub, page template, callout template, styles |

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/examples":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/examples","created":"20260305110542030","modified":"20260305110542030","rdt.order":"4","rdt.plugin-key":"doc-template","rdt.plugin-name":"doc template","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Branded documentation system for rimir plugins","rdt.section":"Examples","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Minimal doc tiddler

The smallest possible doc tiddler — just the required fields and a body:

```
title: $:/plugins/rimir/my-plugin/doc/overview
type: text/vnd.tiddlywiki
rdt.plugin-key: my-plugin
rdt.section: Overview
rdt.order: 1
rdt.plugin-name: My Plugin
rdt.plugin-subtitle: Short description of my plugin
rdt.hub-category: infrastructure
rdt.hub-order: 5
rdt.hub-category-name: Infrastructure

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! What it does

Description here.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
```

!! Non-overview section

Sections other than Overview omit the hub fields:

```
title: $:/plugins/rimir/my-plugin/doc/architecture
type: text/vnd.tiddlywiki
rdt.plugin-key: my-plugin
rdt.section: Architecture
rdt.order: 2
rdt.plugin-name: My Plugin
rdt.plugin-subtitle: Short description of my plugin

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Design decisions

Content here.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
```

!! Callout usage

Four callout types are available:

```
<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="info">
<$fill $name="body">Informational note.</$fill>
</$transclude>

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="warning">
<$fill $name="body">Warning about a gotcha.</$fill>
</$transclude>

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="tip">
<$fill $name="body">Helpful tip.</$fill>
</$transclude>

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="danger">
<$fill $name="body">Critical issue or breaking change.</$fill>
</$transclude>
```

!! readme.tid pattern

Each plugin's `readme.tid` should wrap in `<$tiddler>` to set `currentTiddler` before transcluding the Overview:

```
title: $:/plugins/rimir/my-plugin/readme

<$tiddler tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/my-plugin/doc/overview">
<$transclude tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/my-plugin/doc/overview"/>
</$tiddler>
```

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="warning">
<$fill $name="body">
The `<$tiddler>` wrapper is essential. Without it, `currentTiddler` points to the readme tiddler, and the page template reads the wrong fields.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/faq":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/faq","created":"20260305110542030","modified":"20260305110542030","rdt.order":"5","rdt.plugin-key":"doc-template","rdt.plugin-name":"doc template","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Branded documentation system for rimir plugins","rdt.section":"FAQ","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Q: Why field-based discovery instead of tags?

Tags are global and can collide. Fields like `rdt.section` and `rdt.plugin-key` are namespaced to the doc system, so they won't interfere with other plugins or user tiddlers. Fields also allow richer querying — you can filter by section, plugin, and order in a single filter run.

!! Q: Why does readme.tid need a $tiddler wrapper?

`<$transclude>` does not change `currentTiddler`. When TiddlyWiki renders the plugin's readme (e.g., in the plugin info panel), `currentTiddler` is the readme tiddler itself. The page template reads `rdt.*` fields from `currentTiddler`, so without the `<$tiddler>` wrapper it would find no fields and render nothing.

!! Q: What is the rdt-rendering guard?

When the page template renders tab content, it transcludes the selected tab tiddler. That tiddler's body includes `<$transclude $tiddler="templates/page">` again. Without the guard, this would create infinite recursion. The `rdt-rendering` variable is set to `"yes"` on first render; on the second pass, the template detects this and outputs the raw body slot instead of the full page layout.

!! Q: How does in-place hub navigation work?

The hub stores the selected plugin's Overview tiddler title in `$:/state/rimir/doc-template/hub/selected`. When set, the hub transcludes that tiddler with `rdt-hub-context="yes"`. In this context, all navigation (breadcrumb, footer, related plugins) uses `<$button>` widgets that update the state tiddler instead of `<$link>` widgets that would open new tiddlers.

!! Q: Why use each[] for categories?

The `each[rdt.hub-category]` filter operator deduplicates by field value. Multiple plugins can share a category (e.g., `infrastructure`) — `each[]` ensures the category heading is rendered only once, then all plugins in that category are listed beneath it.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/overview":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/overview","created":"20260305110542030","modified":"20260305110542030","rdt.hub-category":"infrastructure","rdt.hub-category-name":"Infrastructure","rdt.hub-order":"1","rdt.order":"1","rdt.plugin-key":"doc-template","rdt.plugin-name":"doc template","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Branded documentation system for rimir plugins","rdt.section":"Overview","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! What it does

Doc Template provides a unified documentation system for all rimir TiddlyWiki plugins. It renders branded pages with a consistent layout, auto-discovers doc tiddlers via field-based metadata, and organises them into a navigable hub.

!! Features

* ''Branded layout'' — dark header banner with amber accent, author and version badges, breadcrumb navigation
* ''Auto-discovery hub'' — card grid generated from `rdt.*` fields on Overview tiddlers, grouped by category
* ''In-place navigation'' — when opened from the hub, plugin docs navigate via buttons (no new tiddlers); when opened directly, navigation uses standard TW links
* ''Tab system'' — each plugin's doc sections render as tabs, ordered by `rdt.order`
* ''Callout boxes'' — four types (`info`, `warning`, `tip`, `danger`) via a reusable template
* ''Category system'' — fully data-driven; add a new category by setting `rdt.hub-category` + `rdt.hub-category-name` on any Overview tiddler

!! How it works

Each plugin creates doc tiddlers (typically `doc-overview.tid`, `doc-architecture.tid`, etc.) with `rdt.*` fields. The hub discovers all Overview tiddlers, groups them by category, and renders clickable cards. Clicking a card shows that plugin's doc page with tabs for each section.

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="info">
<$fill $name="body">
No tags are needed — discovery is entirely field-based using `rdt.section` and `rdt.plugin-key`.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

!! Plugin files

|!File |!Role |
|`hub.tid` |Hub page — card grid with category grouping |
|`macros.tid` |`rdt.plugin-card` procedure for rendering cards |
|`templates/page.tid` |Main doc page layout (header, breadcrumb, tabs, footer) |
|`templates/callout.tid` |Reusable callout box component |
|`styles.tid` |All CSS for the doc system (`rdt-*` prefix) |

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/prompt":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/prompt","created":"20260305110542031","modified":"20260305110542031","rdt.order":"8","rdt.plugin-key":"doc-template","rdt.plugin-name":"doc template","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Branded documentation system for rimir plugins","rdt.section":"Prompt","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! How to use this prompt

Copy the prompt below and paste it into your conversation when you need documentation created for a new or existing plugin. Replace the placeholder values in the preamble, then let the AI generate all 7 files.

---

!! Prompt

```
Create documentation for the TiddlyWiki plugin described below. Generate exactly 7 .tid files following the established doc-template system.

PLUGIN DETAILS (fill these in):
- Plugin directory: mywiki/plugins/<slug>/
- Plugin key: <slug>
- Display name: <Name>
- Version: <x.y.z>
- Subtitle: <one-line description>
- Hub category: <infrastructure | editor-vcs | ui-framework>
- Hub category name: <Infrastructure | Editor & Version Control | UI Framework & Domain Model>
- Hub order: <number>

Read ALL source files in the plugin directory before writing documentation. Understand the full codebase first.

---

FIELD RULES

Every doc tiddler must have these fields in the header:

    title: $:/plugins/rimir/<slug>/doc/<section>
    type: text/vnd.tiddlywiki
    rdt.plugin-key: <slug>
    rdt.section: <Section Name>
    rdt.order: <1-7>
    rdt.plugin-name: <Display Name>
    rdt.plugin-subtitle: <one-line description>

The Overview tiddler (and ONLY the Overview) also needs:

    rdt.hub-category: <category-key>
    rdt.hub-order: <number>
    rdt.hub-category-name: <Category Display Name>

---

BODY TEMPLATE

Every tiddler body must be wrapped in the page template:

    <$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
    <$fill $name="body">

    ... content here ...

    </$fill>
    </$transclude>

Use `!!` for section headings (H2). Do not use H1 (`!`). Use `!!!` sparingly for subsections only when needed.

---

CALLOUT SYNTAX

Four types available — use where appropriate:

    <$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="info">
    <$fill $name="body">Informational note.</$fill>
    </$transclude>

Types: info (blue, explanatory notes), warning (amber, gotchas/limitations), tip (green, helpful advice), danger (red, critical issues/breaking changes).

---

FILE 1: doc-overview.tid (section: Overview, order: 1)

Purpose: First impression. What this plugin does and why someone would use it.

Required headings and content:
- !! What it does — 1-2 paragraph plain-language summary
- !! Features — bullet list using ''bold'' for feature names, em-dash for description
  Format: * ''Feature name'' — what it does
- !! Prerequisites — what's needed (Node.js server mode, config files, other plugins)
- !! Quick start — numbered steps to get running, with code blocks

Include an info or tip callout for the most important thing a new user should know.

End with a plugin files table:
    |!File |!Role |
    |`filename` |Description |

---

FILE 2: doc-architecture.tid (section: Architecture, order: 2)

Purpose: How the plugin works internally. For developers who need to understand or modify it.

Required headings and content:
- !! Data flow — numbered steps tracing a request/action from trigger to result
  (e.g., "1. Browser: user clicks button" → "2. Widget dispatches message" → ...)
- !! Components — table of files/modules with their layer (Server/Browser/Filesystem) and role
    |!Component |!Layer |!Role |
- !! Design decisions — bullet list explaining WHY, not just what
  Format: * ''Decision'' — rationale
  Cover: security boundaries, performance trade-offs, API choices, data flow direction

Include a warning or danger callout for the single most important architectural gotcha.

---

FILE 3: doc-dev-workflow.tid (section: Dev Workflow, order: 3)

Purpose: Practical guide for working on this plugin day-to-day.

Required headings and content:
- !! Plugin files — table of all files with roles
    |!File |!Role |
- !! Restart rules — table showing which changes need server restart
    |!Change |!Restart needed? |
  Categories: JS modules (always yes), .tid files in plugins (yes — shadow tiddlers),
  external config files (depends), browser-only tiddlers (no if not in plugin)
- !! [Task-specific workflow] — step-by-step for the most common dev task
  (e.g., "Adding a new action", "Defining a new type", "Creating a component")
- !! Testing — how to verify changes (curl commands, browser steps, etc.)
- !! Version history — table of versions and changes
    |!Version |!Changes |

Include a tip callout with the most useful dev shortcut or gotcha workaround.
On Windows, mention: taskkill //F //PID <pid> //T for killing the server.

---

FILE 4: doc-examples.tid (section: Examples, order: 4)

Purpose: Copy-paste recipes. Show, don't tell.

Required content:
- 3-5 practical examples progressing from simple to complex
- Each example gets its own !! heading
- Every example must include a complete code block that can be copied and used
- Show both the configuration/setup AND the usage
- For plugins with APIs: include curl commands with full headers
- For plugins with widgets: include complete wikitext markup
- For plugins with config files: include full JSON examples

Include a warning callout for the most common mistake when using the plugin.

Format for code blocks:
    ```json
    { ... }
    ```
    or
    ```
    <$widget .../>
    ```

---

FILE 5: doc-faq.tid (section: FAQ, order: 5)

Purpose: Answer the questions that come up repeatedly. Address the "why" behind design choices.

Required content:
- 5-7 questions covering:
  1. The most common confusion or mistake
  2. A "why did you do X instead of Y" design rationale question
  3. A security or safety question (if applicable)
  4. A limitations/edge-case question
  5. A "how do I extend/customise this" question

Format:
    !! Q: Question text here?

    Answer in 1-3 paragraphs. Be direct. Start with "Yes", "No", or the key fact.

Do NOT use callouts in FAQ — the Q&A format is sufficient.

---

FILE 6: doc-reference.tid (section: Reference, order: 6)

Purpose: Lookup tables. No prose — just structured data for quick reference.

Required content (include all that apply):
- !! API endpoints — one section per endpoint with method, path, parameters, response codes
    |!Parameter |!Required |!Description |
    |!HTTP |!Condition |
- !! Widget attributes — table per widget
    |!Attribute |!Required |!Description |
- !! Config/schema — JSON structure with field descriptions
    |!Field |!Type |!Description |
- !! State tiddlers — every $:/state/ and $:/temp/ tiddler the plugin uses
    |!Tiddler |!Set by |!Description |
- !! CSS classes — if the plugin defines any
    |!Class |!Element |

Keep descriptions terse (sentence fragments, not full sentences).

---

FILE 7: doc-tw-internals.tid (section: TW Internals, order: 7)

Purpose: TiddlyWiki platform knowledge needed to understand this plugin. For developers unfamiliar with TW internals.

Required content:
- One !! heading per TW concept the plugin relies on
- Explain the TW mechanism first, THEN how this plugin uses it
- Cover: hooks, module types, filter operators, widget patterns, boot sequence,
  state management, syncer behaviour — whatever applies

Typical topics by plugin type:
- Server plugins: route module-type, hooks (th-server-command-post-start),
  exports.before, CSRF validation, skinny vs fat tiddlers
- Browser plugins: action widget pattern, tm-http-request, $:/temp/ tiddlers,
  EditTemplate/ViewTemplate tags, refresh mechanism
- Type/schema plugins: filter operators, $importvariables, shadow tiddler loading,
  plugin.info scanning, all[shadows+tiddlers]

Include a danger callout for any TW behaviour that is surprising or has caused bugs.

---

STYLE RULES

1. Bold: use ''double single quotes'' (TW syntax), not markdown **asterisks**
2. Code: use `backticks` for inline code, triple backticks for blocks
3. Tables: use TW pipe syntax with |!Header| for header cells
4. Lists: use * for bullets, # for numbered steps
5. Callouts: 1-2 per doc file maximum. Don't overuse them.
6. Tone: direct, technical, no fluff. Write for a developer who knows TiddlyWiki basics.
7. Length: aim for 40-80 lines of content per file (excluding template wrapper).
   Overview can be longer (up to 100 lines). Reference can be as long as needed.
8. No H1 headings (single !). Use !! (H2) for sections, !!! (H3) for subsections.
9. Feature bullets: * ''Name'' — description (em-dash, not colon)
10. Don't document what doesn't exist. Only describe actual current behaviour.
```

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/reference":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/reference","created":"20260305110542031","modified":"20260305110542031","rdt.order":"6","rdt.plugin-key":"doc-template","rdt.plugin-name":"doc template","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Branded documentation system for rimir plugins","rdt.section":"Reference","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! rdt.* fields

|!Field |!Used on |!Required |!Description |
|`rdt.plugin-key` |All doc tiddlers |Yes |Plugin identifier for filtering (e.g., `runner`) |
|`rdt.section` |All doc tiddlers |Yes |Section name, used as tab label (e.g., `Overview`, `FAQ`) |
|`rdt.order` |All doc tiddlers |Yes |Tab sort order (numeric string, `1` = first) |
|`rdt.plugin-name` |All doc tiddlers |Yes |Display name in header and cards |
|`rdt.plugin-subtitle` |All doc tiddlers |Yes |Subtitle in header and card description |
|`rdt.hub-category` |Overview only |Yes* |Machine key for hub grouping |
|`rdt.hub-order` |Overview only |Yes* |Numeric sort order within the hub |
|`rdt.hub-category-name` |Overview only |Yes* |Human-readable category heading |

''*'' Required on Overview tiddlers for the plugin to appear in the hub.

!! CSS classes

Shared classes from `$:/plugins/rimir/theme` (prefix `rr-`):

|!Class |!Element |
|`rr-badge-rect` |Base rectangular badge (used with `rdt-badge-author`, `rdt-badge-version`) |
|`rr-callout` / `rr-callout-{info,warning,tip,danger}` |Callout box styles |
|`rr-callout-label` |Callout type label |

Plugin-specific classes (prefix `rdt-`):

|!Class |!Element |
|`rdt-page` |Outer page wrapper (max-width container) |
|`rdt-header` |Dark header banner |
|`rdt-header-title` |Plugin name in header |
|`rdt-header-subtitle` |Plugin subtitle in header |
|`rdt-header-meta` |Badge container |
|`rdt-badge-author` |Author badge color (amber) |
|`rdt-badge-version` |Version badge color (dark) |
|`rdt-breadcrumb` |Breadcrumb bar |
|`rdt-breadcrumb-sep` |Breadcrumb separator (`›`) |
|`rdt-tabs` |Tab bar container |
|`rdt-tab` |Individual tab button |
|`rdt-tab-active` |Active tab highlight (branded amber) |
|`rdt-content` |Content area below tabs |
|`rdt-footer` |Footer section |
|`rdt-link-btn` |Button styled as link (hub context) |
|`rdt-hub-intro` |Hub intro paragraph |
|`rdt-hub-category` |Category heading in hub |
|`rdt-hub-grid` |Card grid container |
|`rdt-card` |Plugin card button |
|`rdt-card-title` |Card plugin name |
|`rdt-card-version` |Card version label |
|`rdt-card-desc` |Card subtitle text |

!! State tiddlers

|!Tiddler |!Set by |!Description |
|`$:/state/rimir/doc-template/hub/selected` |Hub card clicks, navigation buttons |Currently selected plugin doc in hub view |
|`$:/state/rimir/doc-template/tab/<plugin-key>` |Tab clicks |Currently selected tab per plugin |

!! Template parameters

''Page template'' (`$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page`):

* Reads all `rdt.*` fields from `currentTiddler`
* Slot: `body` — the doc section content

''Callout template'' (`$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout`, delegates to `rr-callout` theme classes):

* Parameter `type` — `info` (default), `warning`, `tip`, `danger`
* Parameter `label` — optional override for the callout label (defaults to uppercase of type)
* Slot: `body` — the callout content

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/tw-internals":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/tw-internals","created":"20260305110542031","modified":"20260305110542031","rdt.order":"7","rdt.plugin-key":"doc-template","rdt.plugin-name":"doc template","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Branded documentation system for rimir plugins","rdt.section":"TW Internals","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! $transclude does NOT change currentTiddler

When you write `<$transclude $tiddler="SomeOther"/>`, TiddlyWiki renders that tiddler's content but `currentTiddler` remains unchanged. This is why `readme.tid` needs a `<$tiddler tiddler="...">` wrapper — without it, the page template reads fields from the wrong tiddler.

!! $slot / $fill for content injection

The page template uses `<$slot $name="body">` to define where doc content appears. Callers provide content via `<$fill $name="body">...</$fill>` inside the `<$transclude>`. This is TiddlyWiki's parameterised transclusion mechanism — similar to named slots in component frameworks.

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="info">
<$fill $name="body">
`<$slot>` can only receive `<$fill>` from the immediate `<$transclude>` caller. Nested transclusions cannot pass fills through intermediate levels.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

!! each[] filter for category deduplication

The hub uses `each[rdt.hub-category]` to get one representative tiddler per category. The `each` operator keeps only the first tiddler with a unique value for the given field. Combined with `nsort[rdt.hub-order]`, this ensures categories appear in the intended order.

!! has[] for field-based discovery

`has[rdt.section]` returns all tiddlers that have the `rdt.section` field set (regardless of value). Combined with field-value filters like `rdt.plugin-key<currentPluginKey>`, this forms the discovery mechanism for building tab lists.

!! State tiddler pattern for tabs

Tab state is stored in `$:/state/rimir/doc-template/tab/<plugin-key>`. The `<$button>` widget's `set`/`setTo` attributes write the selected tab tiddler's title into this state tiddler. On render, `selectedTab` reads this state and falls back to `currentTiddler` if the stored value is invalid (no `rdt.section` field).

!! all[shadows+tiddlers] filter

Doc tiddlers are shadow tiddlers (bundled in a plugin). The filter prefix `all[shadows+tiddlers]` ensures both shadow tiddlers and regular tiddlers are searched. Using just `all[tiddlers]` would miss plugin-bundled docs unless they had been overridden.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/documentation":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/documentation","created":"20260305110542031","modified":"20260305110542031","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<%if [[$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template]has[plugin-type]] %>
<$tiddler tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/overview">
<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/doc/overview"/>
</$tiddler>
<%else%>
To display this documentation, the plugin [[$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template]] needs to be installed.

See the [[readme|$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/readme]] tab for a basic overview.
<%endif%>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/hub":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/hub","created":"20260305110542031","modified":"20260305110542031","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$let
  hubState="$:/state/rimir/doc-template/hub/selected"
  hubSelection={{{ [<hubState>get[text]] }}}
>
<%if [<hubSelection>has[rdt.section]] %>
<$tiddler tiddler=<<hubSelection>>>
<$let rdt-hub-context="yes">
<$transclude $tiddler=<<hubSelection>> />
</$let>
</$tiddler>
<%else%>
<div class="rdt-page">

<div class="rdt-header">
<div class="rdt-header-title">rimir Plugin Hub</div>
<div class="rdt-header-subtitle">Documentation for all rimir TiddlyWiki plugins</div>
<div class="rdt-header-meta">
<span class="rr-badge-rect rdt-badge-author">rimir</span>
</div>
</div>

<div class="rdt-content">

<div class="rdt-hub-intro">
A collection of plugins extending TiddlyWiki with filesystem watching, command execution, realm filtering, git integration, reusable components, a type system, and organisational modelling.
</div>

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/theme/callout" type="tip" label="Install">
<$fill $name="body">
Drag any card into another ~TiddlyWiki browser tab to install the plugin.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

<$list filter="[all[shadows+tiddlers]rdt.section[Overview]has[rdt.hub-category]nsort[rdt.hub-order]each[rdt.hub-category]]" variable="catTiddler">
<$let cat={{{ [<catTiddler>get[rdt.hub-category]] }}} catName={{{ [<catTiddler>get[rdt.hub-category-name]] }}}>
<div class="rdt-hub-category"><$text text=<<catName>>/></div>
<div class="rdt-hub-grid">
<$list filter="[all[shadows+tiddlers]rdt.section[Overview]rdt.hub-category<cat>sort[rdt.plugin-name]]">
<$macrocall $name="rdt.plugin-card" title=<<currentTiddler>>/>
</$list>
</div>
</$let>
</$list>

</div>
</div>
<%endif%>
</$let>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/icon":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/icon","created":"20260305110542033","modified":"20260305110542033","type":"image/svg+xml","text":"<svg width="22pt" height="22pt" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="maroon" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
<path d="M14 2H6a2 2 0 0 0-2 2v16a2 2 0 0 0 2 2h12a2 2 0 0 0 2-2V8z"/>
<polyline points="14 2 14 8 20 8"/>
<line x1="8" y1="13" x2="16" y2="13"/>
<line x1="8" y1="17" x2="13" y2="17"/>
</svg>"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/macros":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/macros","created":"20260305110542033","modified":"20260305110542033","tags":"$:/tags/Macro","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"\procedure rdt.plugin-card(title)
<$let cardPluginKey={{{ [<title>get[rdt.plugin-key]] }}} cardPluginTitle={{{ [[$:/plugins/rimir/]addsuffix<cardPluginKey>] }}}>
<$draggable tiddler=<<cardPluginTitle>> class="rdt-card-draggable">
<$button class="tc-btn-invisible rdt-card" set="$:/state/rimir/doc-template/hub/selected" setTo=<<title>>>
<div class="rdt-card-title"><$text text={{{ [<title>get[rdt.plugin-name]] }}}/><span class="rdt-card-version">v<$text text={{{ [<cardPluginTitle>get[version]] }}}/></span></div>
<div class="rdt-card-desc"><$text text={{{ [<title>get[rdt.plugin-subtitle]] }}}/></div>
</$button>
</$draggable>
</$let>
\end
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/readme-structure":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/readme-structure","created":"20260305110542033","modified":"20260305110542033","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"! Readme Structure Guide

Every rimir plugin's `readme.tid` must follow this structure. The readme is the first tab users see in the plugin detail view and should provide enough information to decide whether to install the plugin.

!! Required sections

!!! 1. Title and description

The `!` heading should be the plugin's display name (lowercase). Follow with one to two sentences describing what the plugin does.

!!! 2. Key features

A bullet list of the most important features, each with a bold label and brief description:

```
* ''Feature name'' — what it does
```

!!! 3. Prerequisites

List any external dependencies, required tools, or other plugins. If none, state "No external prerequisites."

!!! 4. Quick start

Minimal instructions to get started — install steps, basic usage example, or first configuration action.

!! Example

```
! my plugin

One sentence describing the plugin purpose.

!! Key features

* ''Feature A'' — does something useful
* ''Feature B'' — does something else

!! Prerequisites

* some-tool (required for feature B)

!! Quick start

Install plugin. Use `<$my-widget/>` in any tiddler.
```
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/readme":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/readme","created":"20260305110542033","modified":"20260305110542033","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"! doc template

Branded documentation system for all rimir plugins. Renders plugin documentation with a consistent layout featuring a dark header, tab navigation, breadcrumbs, and an auto-discovery hub that lists all documented plugins.

!! Key features

* ''Auto-discovery hub'' — card grid grouped by category, finds all documented plugins automatically
* ''Branded page layout'' — header, breadcrumb, tabs, and footer for consistent presentation
* ''Field-based discovery'' — no tags needed, uses `rdt.*` fields for metadata
* ''Callout boxes'' — four types: info, warning, tip, danger
* ''In-place navigation'' — seamless browsing when accessed from the hub
* ''Related plugins footer'' — links to related plugins at the bottom of each page

!! Prerequisites

* theme plugin (for CSS classes)

!! Quick start

Create `doc-overview.tid` in your plugin with `rdt.plugin-key`, `rdt.section`, `rdt.plugin-name`, `rdt.plugin-subtitle`, `rdt.hub-category` fields. The version is read automatically from `plugin.info`. Transclude `$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page` and fill the `body` slot. The hub auto-discovers it.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/settings":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/settings","created":"20260305110542033","modified":"20260305110542033","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<div class="rr-settings-info-card">
No configurable settings. Future settings will appear here.
</div>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/styles":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/styles","created":"20260305110542033","modified":"20260305110542033","tags":"$:/tags/Stylesheet","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"/* ============================================================
   rimir Doc Template — Branded Documentation Styles
   Prefix: rdt-
   ============================================================ */

/* --- Page wrapper --- */
.rdt-page {
    max-width: 1500px;
    margin: 0 auto;
}

/* --- Header banner --- */
.rdt-header {
    background: #1e293b;
    border-top: 4px solid #d97706;
    border-radius: 6px 6px 0 0;
    padding: 20px 24px;
    margin-bottom: 0;
    color: #fff;
}

.rdt-header-title {
    font-size: 1.5em;
    font-weight: 700;
    margin: 0 0 4px 0;
    color: #fff;
}

.rdt-header-subtitle {
    font-size: 0.95em;
    color: #94a3b8;
    margin: 0;
}

.rdt-header-meta {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 12px;
    margin-top: 10px;
}

.rdt-badge-author {
    background: #d97706;
    color: #fff;
}

.rdt-badge-version {
    background: #334155;
    color: #cbd5e1;
}

/* --- Breadcrumb --- */
.rdt-breadcrumb {
    font-size: 0.85em;
    color: <<colour muted-foreground>>;
    padding: 8px 24px;
    background: <<colour page-background>>;
    border-left: 1px solid <<colour tiddler-border>>;
    border-right: 1px solid <<colour tiddler-border>>;
}

.rdt-breadcrumb a {
    color: #d97706;
    text-decoration: none;
}

.rdt-breadcrumb a:hover {
    text-decoration: underline;
}

.rdt-breadcrumb-sep {
    margin: 0 6px;
    color: <<colour muted-foreground>>;
}

/* --- Tab navigation --- */
.rdt-tabs {
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    gap: 0;
    border-bottom: 2px solid <<colour tiddler-border>>;
    background: <<colour page-background>>;
    padding: 0 24px;
    border-left: 1px solid <<colour tiddler-border>>;
    border-right: 1px solid <<colour tiddler-border>>;
}

.rdt-tab {
    padding: 8px 16px;
    font-size: 0.9em;
    font-weight: 500;
    color: <<colour muted-foreground>>;
    text-decoration: none;
    border-bottom: 2px solid transparent;
    margin-bottom: -2px;
    transition: color 0.15s, border-color 0.15s;
    cursor: pointer;
    background: none;
    border-top: none;
    border-left: none;
    border-right: none;
    font-family: inherit;
    line-height: inherit;
}

.rdt-tab:hover {
    color: <<colour foreground>>;
    text-decoration: none;
}

.rdt-tab-active {
    color: #d97706;
    border-bottom-color: #d97706;
    font-weight: 600;
}

/* --- Content area --- */
.rdt-content {
    padding: 24px;
    background: <<colour tiddler-background>>;
    border-left: 1px solid <<colour tiddler-border>>;
    border-right: 1px solid <<colour tiddler-border>>;
    border-bottom: 1px solid <<colour tiddler-border>>;
    border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px;
    line-height: 1.6;
}

.rdt-content h2 {
    font-size: 1.25em;
    font-weight: 600;
    margin: 1.5em 0 0.5em 0;
    padding-bottom: 4px;
    border-bottom: 1px solid <<colour tiddler-border>>;
    color: <<colour foreground>>;
}

.rdt-content h2:first-child {
    margin-top: 0;
}

.rdt-content h3 {
    font-size: 1.05em;
    font-weight: 600;
    margin: 1.2em 0 0.4em 0;
    color: <<colour foreground>>;
}

.rdt-content table {
    width: 100%;
    border-collapse: collapse;
    margin: 1em 0;
    font-size: 0.9em;
}

.rdt-content th {
    text-align: left;
    padding: 8px 12px;
    background: <<colour table-header-background>>;
    border: 1px solid <<colour table-border>>;
    font-weight: 600;
}

.rdt-content td {
    padding: 8px 12px;
    border: 1px solid <<colour table-border>>;
    vertical-align: top;
}

.rdt-content code {
    font-size: 0.9em;
    padding: 1px 5px;
    border-radius: 3px;
    background: <<colour code-background>>;
    color: <<colour code-foreground>>;
}

.rdt-content pre {
    padding: 12px 16px;
    border-radius: 4px;
    overflow-x: auto;
    font-size: 0.85em;
    background: <<colour code-background>>;
}

/* --- Footer --- */
.rdt-footer {
    margin-top: 24px;
    padding-top: 16px;
    border-top: 1px solid <<colour tiddler-border>>;
    font-size: 0.85em;
    color: <<colour muted-foreground>>;
}

.rdt-footer-heading {
    font-weight: 600;
    margin-bottom: 6px;
    color: <<colour foreground>>;
}

.rdt-footer-links {
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    gap: 8px 16px;
}

.rdt-footer-links a,
.rdt-footer-links .rdt-link-btn {
    color: #d97706;
    text-decoration: none;
}

.rdt-footer-links a:hover,
.rdt-footer-links .rdt-link-btn:hover {
    text-decoration: underline;
}

.rdt-footer-hub {
    margin-top: 12px;
}

.rdt-footer-hub a,
.rdt-footer-hub .rdt-link-btn {
    color: #d97706;
    text-decoration: none;
    font-weight: 500;
}

/* --- Button-as-link (hub context) --- */
.rdt-link-btn {
    color: #d97706;
    text-decoration: none;
    cursor: pointer;
    font-size: inherit;
    font-family: inherit;
}

.rdt-link-btn:hover {
    text-decoration: underline;
}

/* --- Hub page --- */
.rdt-hub-intro {
    font-size: 1.05em;
    color: <<colour muted-foreground>>;
    margin-bottom: 24px;
}

.rdt-hub-category {
    font-size: 1.1em;
    font-weight: 600;
    margin: 24px 0 12px 0;
    color: <<colour foreground>>;
}

.rdt-hub-category:first-of-type {
    margin-top: 0;
}

.rdt-hub-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(260px, 1fr));
    gap: 12px;
    margin-bottom: 24px;
}

.rdt-card {
    border: 1px solid <<colour tiddler-border>>;
    border-radius: 6px;
    padding: 16px;
    background: <<colour tiddler-background>>;
    transition: border-color 0.15s, box-shadow 0.15s;
    cursor: pointer;
    text-decoration: none;
    display: block;
    color: <<colour foreground>>;
}

.rdt-card:hover {
    border-color: #d97706;
    box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(217, 119, 6, 0.1);
    text-decoration: none;
    color: <<colour foreground>>;
}

.rdt-card-title {
    font-size: 1.05em;
    font-weight: 600;
    margin-bottom: 4px;
}

.rdt-card-version {
    font-size: 0.75em;
    color: <<colour muted-foreground>>;
    font-weight: normal;
    margin-left: 6px;
}

.rdt-card-desc {
    font-size: 0.88em;
    color: <<colour muted-foreground>>;
    line-height: 1.4;
}

/* --- Draggable cards --- */
.rdt-card-draggable {
    cursor: grab;
}

.rdt-card-draggable.tc-dragging {
    opacity: 0.4;
    cursor: grabbing;
}

.rdt-card-draggable .rdt-card {
    cursor: inherit;
}
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout","created":"20260305110542034","modified":"20260305110542034","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"\parameters (type:"info", label:"")

<$let
  calloutClass={{{ [[rr-callout rr-callout-]addsuffix<type>] }}}
  resolvedLabel={{{ [<label>!is[blank]] :else[<type>uppercase[]] }}}
>
<div class=<<calloutClass>>>
<div class="rr-callout-label"><$text text=<<resolvedLabel>>/></div>
<$slot $name="body">
//No callout content.//
</$slot>
</div>
</$let>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page","created":"20260305110542034","modified":"20260305110542034","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"\procedure rdt-render-tabs()
<$list filter="[all[shadows+tiddlers]has[rdt.section]rdt.plugin-key<currentPluginKey>nsort[rdt.order]]" variable="tab-tiddler">
<$let tabSection={{{ [<tab-tiddler>get[rdt.section]] }}} isActive={{{ [<tab-tiddler>match<selectedTab>then[rdt-tab rdt-tab-active]else[rdt-tab]] }}}>
<$button class=<<isActive>> set=<<tabState>> setTo=<<tab-tiddler>>><<tabSection>></$button>
</$let>
</$list>
\end

\procedure rdt-render-footer()
<div class="rdt-footer">
<div class="rdt-footer-heading">Related plugins</div>
<div class="rdt-footer-links">
<$list filter="[all[shadows+tiddlers]has[rdt.section]rdt.section[Overview]!rdt.plugin-key<currentPluginKey>sort[rdt.plugin-name]]" variable="related">
<%if [<rdt-hub-context>match[yes]] %>
<$button class="tc-btn-invisible rdt-link-btn" set="$:/state/rimir/doc-template/hub/selected" setTo=<<related>>><$text text={{{ [<related>get[rdt.plugin-name]] }}}/></$button>
<%else%>
<$link to=<<related>>><$text text={{{ [<related>get[rdt.plugin-name]] }}}/></$link>
<%endif%>
</$list>
</div>
<div class="rdt-footer-hub">
<%if [<rdt-hub-context>match[yes]] %>
<$button class="tc-btn-invisible rdt-link-btn" set="$:/state/rimir/doc-template/hub/selected" setTo="">← Back to Plugin Hub</$button>
<%else%>
<$link to="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/hub">← Back to Plugin Hub</$link>
<%endif%>
</div>
</div>
\end

<%if [<rdt-rendering>match[yes]] %>
<$slot $name="body">
//No content provided.//
</$slot>
<%else%>
<$let
  rdt-rendering="yes"
  currentPluginKey={{{ [<currentTiddler>get[rdt.plugin-key]] }}}
  currentPluginName={{{ [<currentTiddler>get[rdt.plugin-name]] }}}
  currentPluginVersion={{{ [[$:/plugins/rimir/]addsuffix<currentPluginKey>get[version]] }}}
  currentPluginSubtitle={{{ [<currentTiddler>get[rdt.plugin-subtitle]] }}}
  tabState={{{ [[$:/state/rimir/doc-template/tab/]addsuffix{!!rdt.plugin-key}] }}}
  selectedTab={{{ [<tabState>get[text]has[rdt.section]] :else[<currentTiddler>] }}}
  currentSection={{{ [<selectedTab>get[rdt.section]] }}}
>
<div class="rdt-page">

<!-- Header -->
<div class="rdt-header">
<div class="rdt-header-title"><$text text=<<currentPluginName>>/></div>
<div class="rdt-header-subtitle"><$text text=<<currentPluginSubtitle>>/></div>
<div class="rdt-header-meta">
<span class="rr-badge-rect rdt-badge-author">rimir</span>
<span class="rr-badge-rect rdt-badge-version">v<$text text=<<currentPluginVersion>>/></span>
</div>
</div>

<!-- Breadcrumb -->
<div class="rdt-breadcrumb">
<%if [<rdt-hub-context>match[yes]] %>
<$button class="tc-btn-invisible rdt-link-btn" set="$:/state/rimir/doc-template/hub/selected" setTo="">Hub</$button>
<%else%>
<$link to="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/hub">Hub</$link>
<%endif%>
<span class="rdt-breadcrumb-sep">›</span>
<$text text=<<currentPluginName>>/>
<span class="rdt-breadcrumb-sep">›</span>
<$text text=<<currentSection>>/>
</div>

<!-- Tabs -->
<div class="rdt-tabs">
<<rdt-render-tabs>>
</div>

<!-- Content -->
<div class="rdt-content">
<$transclude $tiddler=<<selectedTab>> />

<<rdt-render-footer>>
</div>

</div>
</$let>
<%endif%>
"}}}
